records.ety: [L. deprehendere, deprehensum; de- + prehendere to lay hold of, seize. See Prehensile.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deprehended; p. pr. & vb. n. Deprehending.]

defs: To take unawares or by surprise; to seize, as a person committing an unlawful act; to catch; to apprehend.
To detect; to discover; to find out.
quotes: The motion . . . are to be deprehended by experience.
